channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Two decades on, Iraqis and US veterans face the enduring scars of the Iraq War
item.description: This SBS Dateline feature explores the lasting impact of the Iraq War on both Iraqi civilians and US veterans, twenty years after the 2003 invasion. The documentary brings together Iraqis who lived th...

item.title: Inflation rises to 4.1% in March due to higher fuel and transport costs
item.description: The Philippines experienced a surge in inflation in March 2026, reaching 4.1% as fuel and transport costs rose significantly. The increase in consumer prices comes after a period of relatively lower i...

item.title: Candidates Disqualified at Final Interview Despite Meeting Akwa Ibom Civil Service Requirements
item.description: Some candidates wey qualify for the Akwa Ibom State Civil Service job don yan say dem no allow dem enter final interview even tho dem meet all the advertised requirements. The matter involve three gra...

item.title: Claims of excessive police violence at illegal rave in Dorset
item.description: Police in Dorset are facing accusations of using excessive force to break up an illegal rave at East Lulworth, which had attracted around 2,000 people. The event, held on Sunday evening, was shut down...

item.title: MQM-P's Request for Extension in Intra-Party Elections Denied by ECP
item.description: The Election Commission of Pakistan (ECP) has rejected a request from the Muttahida Qaumi Movement-Pakistan (MQM-P) seeking a two-month extension to comply with directives related to their intra-party...

item.title: UN Security Council to vote on compromise resolution on Strait of Hormuz shipping protection
item.description: The United Nations Security Council is scheduled to vote on Tuesday, April 7, 2026, on a resolution addressing the safety of commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The move comes amid ongoing ho...

item.title: DP Kindiki Urges Mt Kenya to Back Ruto’s 2027 Re-election for 2032 Power Bid
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has called on residents from the Mt Kenya region to rally behind President William Ruto in his bid for re-election in 2027, saying this will strengthen the region’s ch...

item.title: Adriana Rivas to be extradited to Chile over alleged Pinochet-era kidnappings
item.description: Adriana Elcira Rivas, a former nanny from Sydney, is set to be extradited to Chile after losing her final appeal in the Federal Court. Rivas, who emigrated to Australia in the late 1970s, is accused o...

item.title: Essential Cleaning Steps to Prepare Your Patio for Spring
item.description: The Global News article by Melissa Maker offers a practical guide to getting your patio or deck truly ready for spring. While many people focus on setting up outdoor furniture and decor, Maker emphasi...

item.title: Christian, Reiven, JM, and Khimo Take the ‘LISTEN’ Stage on Showtime
item.description: Christian, Reiven, JM, and Khimo, four popular personalities who gained fame on TikTok, recently took the stage on ‘It’s Showtime’ to perform the song ‘LISTEN’ live. Their performance showcased their ...

item.title: Finance Minister Highlights Global Risks of US-Iran Conflict
item.description: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has cautioned that the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran has escalated beyond a regional matter, presenting significant risks to the global econom...

item.title: Freak storm in KwaBhaca leaves families homeless and claims two lives
item.description: A severe freak storm struck Ngwetsheni village in KwaBhaca, Eastern Cape, on Sunday, causing extensive destruction and claiming the lives of two men. According to Umzimvubu mayor Zukiswa Ndevu, the me...

item.title: PAGASA reports 42°C heat index posing health risks in Occidental Mindoro and Iloilo
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) has issued a warning after recording a heat index reaching 42°C in Occidental Mindoro and Iloilo on April 7, 2...

item.title: The Purging of Women in Trump’s Administration: Bondi and Noem’s Removal
item.description: The recent dismissal of former Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem and former Attorney General Pam Bondi by President Trump highlights a troubling trend of purging women from positions of power wi...

item.title: UN Security Council to Vote on Measures Amid Iran-Israel Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The UN Security Council is scheduled to vote on April 7, 2026, regarding Iran's actions threatening the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane for global oil and gas. The draft resolution calls for I...

item.title: Nigeria Strengthens Its Role as a Leading Aviation and Commercial Hub in Africa
item.description: Olubunmi Kuku, wey be Managing Director of Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria (FAAN), don talk say Nigeria dey emerge as one of Africa’s major aviation and commercial hubs. She yan dis one for ACI ...

item.title: Citrini Research Sends Analyst to Hormuz to Assess Oil Shipping Activity
item.description: Citrini Research, a small and relatively unknown Wall Street firm, has made waves once again, this time by dispatching one of its analysts to the heart of the Middle East. The firm had previously caus...

item.title: Majembe Wins Vurugu Match Against Mbavu Destroyer and Responds to Post-Fight Claims
item.description: Amateur boxer Majembe, whose real name is Portifas Odipo, has finally commented on his victory over Ferdinand Omondi, popularly known as Mbavu Destroyer, in the highly anticipated Vurugu fight at Kasa...

item.title: Patrick Radden Keefe investigates the tragic death of Zac Brettler and London’s culture of wealth and deception
item.description: Patrick Radden Keefe, the acclaimed American journalist and author of 'Say Nothing' and 'Empire of Pain', explores the unsettling death of 19-year-old Zac Brettler in his latest book, 'London Falling'...

item.title: Rise in e-bike and e-scooter battery fires highlights need for stricter safety standards
item.description: Fire incidents involving electric bikes and scooters surged to record levels across the United Kingdom in 2025, according to figures obtained through Freedom of Information requests. The data reveal t...
